description: An advanced study of global economic interactions, covering classical and modern trade theories, trade policies, welfare effects, and international financial markets.

topics.name: Unit 1: Comparative Advantage
topics.description: Foundations of international trade, opportunity cost, absolute advantage, and the gains from specialization.

topics.name: Unit 2: The Ricardian Model
topics.description: One-factor trade models, production possibility frontiers, autarky vs. trade equilibrium, and relative price determination.

topics.name: Unit 3: Ricardian Model of Welfare
topics.description: Analyzing welfare effects, Pareto efficiency in trade, and the impact of factor mobility on national income.

topics.name: Unit 4: Immobile Factor Model
topics.description: Short-run trade impacts where resources cannot shift easily, homothetic preferences, and income redistribution.

topics.name: Unit 5: Heckscher-Ohlin Model
topics.description: Factor proportions theory, labor vs. capital intensity, and how resource abundance dictates global export patterns.

topics.name: Unit 6: Specific Factors & Dynamics
topics.description: The impact of trade on specific factor owners, capital income shifts, and the long-term dynamics of labor wages.

topics.name: Unit 7: New Trade Theory & Scale
topics.description: Economies of scale, monopolistic competition, and why similar countries trade similar products (Intra-industry trade).

topics.name: Unit 8: Effects of Trade Policies
topics.description: Large vs. small country assumptions, graphical welfare analysis, and the mechanics of free trade equilibrium.

topics.name: Unit 9: Tariffs & Trade Wars
topics.description: Specific vs. Ad Valorem tariffs, welfare deadweight loss, and the economic consequences of retaliation.

topics.name: Unit 10: Quotas & Non-Tariff Barriers
topics.description: Import quotas, voluntary export restraints, export subsidies, and domestic policy interventions.

topics.name: Unit 11: Financial Aspects of Trade
topics.description: The foreign exchange market, currency valuation, trade accounts, and the impact of government deficits on trade balance.

topics.name: Unit 12: Exchange Rates & Investment
topics.description: Interest Rate Parity (IRP), arbitrage, hedging, and the relationship between international investment and rates of return.
